key_insight: An autonomous LLM coding agent improved a crystal graph network for band-gap prediction by assembling known modeling ideas into a higher-performing expert-designed system.
engineering_relevance: This suggests AI agents can accelerate materials informatics model development for structure-to-property prediction, especially on benchmarked tasks with clear metrics and large crystal datasets.
method: Used a crystal graph neural network for predicting band gaps from crystal structures
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges
Incorporated a crystal space-group embedding and other known ML improvements
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with over 100,000 crystals, the agent-built model trained without external pretraining outperformed all 17 expert-designed models reported for the task.
watch_item: The gains came from recombining established methods rather than discovering fundamentally new physics or architectures, so autonomous optimization may be limited by existing design space and benchmark overfitting.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
